data.title: U.S. Secretary of Commerce: China and the United States have had initial contact on tariff issues through intermediaries
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Phoenix TV, U.S. Commerce Secretary Lutnick said in an interview with ABC that the United States and China have had "preliminary contacts" on tariffs through intermediaries. At the same time, he pointed out that the U.S. government is studying and formulating special tariff measures for key electronic products such as semiconductors.

data.title: Binance responds to OM flash crash: mainly caused by cross-trading platform liquidation, will closely monitor developments
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Binance officially announced that it has noticed that the MANTRA token OM has experienced drastic price fluctuations. Preliminary investigations have found that the fluctuations in the past day were mainly caused by cross-trading platform liquidations.
Since October last year, Binance has implemented a number of risk control measures for OM tokens, including reducing leverage levels. Binance will continue to monitor leverage levels and adjust them according to market conditions to strengthen risk control and help reduce volatility.
Since January this year, Binance has also enabled pop-up prompts for OM on the spot trading page, reminding users that the token economic model of the token has changed significantly and the supply has increased.
Binance will continue to pay close attention to developments and take appropriate actions to protect user interests and maintain the stability and fairness of the platform.

data.title: U.S. and China Engage in Preliminary Tariff Discussions
data.description: According to Foresight News, U.S. Commerce Secretary Lutnick revealed in an interview with ABC that the United States and China have initiated preliminary discussions on tariff issues through intermediaries. He also mentioned that the U.S. government is considering special tariff measures targeting key electronic products, including semiconductors.

data.title: RFC market value exceeds 100 million US dollars, and early large investors have made a profit of more than 1.3 million US dollars
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of on-chain data analyst @ai_9684xtpa, the market value of RFC has exceeded 100 million US dollars. Since April 7, a certain address that once topped the holding list has bought 16.21 million RFCs at an average price of 0.0174 US dollars and has not sold them so far. The current floating profit has exceeded 1.3 million US dollars, with a return rate of 446.4%.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETF had a net outflow of $82.47 million last week, marking seven consecutive weeks of net outflows
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to SoSoValue data, last week's trading day (April 7 to April 11, Eastern Time), the Ethereum spot ETF had a net outflow of $82.47 million last week.
The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest weekly net inflow last week was Blackrock's Ethereum ETF ETHA, with a weekly net inflow of $977,000. Currently, ETHA's total net inflow has reached $4.05 billion.
The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest weekly net outflow last week was Fidelity's Ethereum ETF FETH, with a weekly net outflow of $45.04 million. Currently, FETH's total net inflow has reached $1.37 billion. The second largest was Grayscale's Ethereum Trust ETF ETHE, with a weekly net outflow of $28.32 million. Currently, ETHE's total net outflow has reached $4.23 billion.
As of press time, the total net asset value of the Ethereum spot ETF is US$5.24 billion, the ETF net asset ratio (market value to the total market value of Ethereum) is 2.76%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached US$2.28 billion.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs had a net outflow of $713 million last week, with BlackRock's Bitcoin ETF IBIT leading the way with a weekly net outflow of $342 million
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to SoSoValue data, last week's trading day (April 7 to April 11, Eastern Time), Bitcoin spot ETFs had a net outflow of US$713 million last week.
The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest weekly net inflow last week was Grayscale Bitcoin Mini Trust ETF BTC, with a weekly net inflow of US$2.39 million, and the current total net inflow of BTC has reached US$1.15 billion.
The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest weekly net outflow last week was Blackrock Bitcoin ETF IBIT, with a weekly net outflow of US$343 million, and the current total net inflow of IBIT has reached US$39.57 billion. The second is Grayscale Bitcoin ETF GBTC, with a weekly net outflow of US$161 million, and the current total net outflow of GBTC has reached US$22.78 billion.
As of press time, the total net asset value of the Bitcoin spot ETF is US$93.36 billion, the ETF net asset ratio (market value to the total market value of Bitcoin) is 5.61%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached US$35.36 billion.

data.title: Market Sentiment Shifts as Funding Rates Indicate Neutral Outlook
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, recent data from Coinglass reveals that the funding rates on major centralized and decentralized exchanges no longer suggest a predominantly bearish market sentiment. The funding rates for key cryptocurrencies are detailed in the accompanying data.

Funding rates are fees set by cryptocurrency trading platforms to maintain the balance between contract prices and the prices of underlying assets, typically applied to perpetual contracts. This mechanism facilitates the exchange of funds between long and short traders, without the platform charging this fee, to adjust the cost or profit of holding contracts, ensuring contract prices remain close to the asset prices.

A funding rate of 0.01% indicates a baseline rate. When the funding rate exceeds 0.01%, it signifies a generally bullish market sentiment. Conversely, a funding rate below 0.005% indicates a bearish market sentiment.

data.title: Bitcoin Spot ETFs Experience Significant Weekly Outflows
data.description:
According to Foresight News, data from SoSoValue indicates that Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a net outflow of $713 million during the trading week from April 7 to April 11, 2025, Eastern Time. The Grayscale Bitcoin Mini Trust ETF BTC recorded the highest weekly net inflow among Bitcoin spot ETFs, amounting to $2.39 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $1.15 billion.

Conversely, the Blackrock Bitcoin ETF IBIT experienced the largest weekly net outflow, totaling $343 million, with its historical total net inflow reaching $39.57 billion. The Grayscale Bitcoin ETF GBTC followed with a weekly net outflow of $161 million, contributing to a historical total net outflow of $22.78 billion.

As of the time of reporting, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs stands at $93.36 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio of 5.61% compared to Bitcoin's total market capitalization. The cumulative historical net inflow for Bitcoin spot ETFs has reached $35.36 billion.

data.title: Eric Trump: Bitcoin is one of the most valuable stores of value with instant liquidity
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Trump's second son Eric Trump recently said, "Bitcoin is one of the most valuable means of storage, has instant liquidity, and is an incredible real estate hedging tool."

data.title: LBank has launched BANG U-margined perpetual contracts
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the official announcement, LBank has launched the BANG U-based perpetual contract at 11:40 (SGT) on April 12, 2025, with a maximum leverage of 20 times.
